rows.note: Compiled rules run offline against your diff — zero LLM calls in the gate. Wall time is receipted below, not asserted.
pre-commit / pre-push / post-merge / post-checkout. Installed explicitly — never silently.
Plain markdown files are the canonical source; the search index is a derived, rebuildable cache.
Requires one IDE-level MCP registration step (see the MCP setup guide). The vector store is derived — deleting it loses nothing canonical.
Parked under a standing freeze since 2026-05-17 (receipt below) pending a replacement derivation pipeline. The compiled corpus remains fully enforced.

rows.mechanism: AI review lanes with lesson grounding (`totem review`)
rows.note: LLM-assisted and advisory — verdicts record which lessons were consulted. The deterministic gates above are the floor; this never replaces them.
Proposes rule fixes as a pull request; a human merges every change. Nothing lands autonomously.

rows.mechanism: Claim-discipline gates on our own docs
rows.note: README badges and this page are derived from committed data and drift-gated in CI. A claim whose anchor stops resolving fails the build.

A committed exhibit of the loop: one real mistake, the rule compiled from its lesson, and CI re-proving on every pull request that the mistake stays blocked — zero LLM calls. Timing lives in the committed receipt beside the parameters that produced it.

local-only · multi-seat opt-in. File-anchored mailboxes for repos where several agents share one working tree; the single-seat path never touches it. Bring your own orchestration — this is coordination state, not a runtime.

rows.mechanism: Rule derivation from merged-PR history, validated against held-out controls
rows.note: Goal: replace the parked legacy compiler with derivation that passes held-out validation before any public claim rides on it. A prototype exists; its runs to date are under-powered, so this stays a goal row.
